AlgebraicStructureTraits.name: AlgebraicStructureTraits
AlgebraicStructureTraits.brief: A model of AlgebraicStructureTraits reflects the algebraic structure of an associated type Type .
AlgebraicStructureTraits.detailed: Depending on the concepts that Type fulfills, it contains various functors and descriptive tags. Moreover it gives access to the several possible algebraic operations within that structure. IntegralDomainWithoutDivision IntegralDomain UniqueFactorizationDomain EuclideanRing Field FieldWithSqrt FieldWithKthRoot FieldWithRootOf CGAL::Integral_domain_without_division_tag CGAL::Integral_domain_tag CGAL::Unique_factorization_domain_tag CGAL::Euclidean_ring_tag CGAL::Field_tag CGAL::Field_with_sqrt_tag CGAL::Field_with_kth_root_tag CGAL::Field_with_root_of_tag Has models CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits <T>

members.name: Algebraic_category
members.brief: Tag indicating the algebraic structure of the associated type.
members.detailed: Tag is: Type is model of: CGAL::Null_tag no algebraic concept CGAL::Integral_domain_without_division_tag IntegralDomainWithoutDivision CGAL::Integral_domain_tag IntegralDomain CGAL::Unique_factorization_domain_tag UniqueFactorizationDomain CGAL::Euclidean_ring_tag EuclideanRing CGAL::Field_tag Field CGAL::Field_with_sqrt_tag FieldWithSqrt CGAL::Field_with_kth_root_tag FieldWithKthRoot CGAL::Field_with_root_of_tag FieldWithRootOf

members.name: Is_exact
members.brief: Tag indicating whether Type is exact.
members.detailed: This is either CGAL::Tag_true or CGAL::Tag_false . An algebraic structure is considered exact, if all operations required by its concept are computed such that a comparison of two algebraic expressions is always correct. The exactness covers only those operations that are required by the algebraic structure concept. e.g. an exact Field may have a Sqrt functor that is not exact.

members.name: Is_numerical_sensitive
members.brief: Tag indicating whether Type is numerical sensitive.
members.detailed: This is either CGAL::Tag_true or CGAL::Tag_false . An algebraic structure is considered as numerically sensitive, if the performance of the type is sensitive to the condition number of an algorithm.

members.name: Boolean
members.brief: This type specifies the return type of the predicates provided by this traits.
members.detailed: The type must be convertible to bool and typically the type indeed maps to bool . However, there are also cases such as interval arithmetic, in which it is CGAL::Uncertain<bool> or some similar type.

members.name: Is_zero
members.detailed: Required by the concept IntegralDomainWithoutDivision . In case Type is also model of RealEmbeddable this is a model of RealEmbeddableTraits_::IsZero .

AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Div.name: AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Div
AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Div.brief: AdaptableBinaryFunction computes the integral quotient of division with remainder.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: This operator is defined if NT1 and NT2 are ExplicitInteroperable with coercion type AlgebraicStructureTraits::Type .

AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Divides.name: AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Divides
AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Divides.brief: AdaptableBinaryFunction , returns true if the first argument divides the second argument.
AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Divides.detailed: Integral division (a.k.a. exact division or division without remainder) maps ring elements $ (n,d)$ to ring element $ c$ such that $ n = dc$ if such a $ c$ exists. In this case it is said that $ d$ divides $ n$ . This functor is required to provide two operators. The first operator takes two arguments and returns true if the first argument divides the second argument. The second operator returns $ c$ via the additional third argument. Refines AdaptableBinaryFunction AlgebraicStructureTraits AlgebraicStructureTraits_::IntegralDivision

members.name: operator()
members.detailed: Moreover it computes $ c$ if $ d$ divides $ n$ , otherwise the value of $ c$ is undefined.

AlgebraicStructureTraits_::DivMod.name: AlgebraicStructureTraits_::DivMod
AlgebraicStructureTraits_::DivMod.brief: AdaptableFunctor computes both integral quotient and remainder of division with remainder. The quotient $ q$ and remainder $ r$ are computed such that $ x = q*y + r$ and $ |r| < |y|$ with respect to the proper integer norm of the represented ring. For integers this norm is the absolute value. For univariate polynomials this norm is the degree. In particular, $ r$ is chosen to be $ 0$ if possible. Moreover, we require $ q$ to be minimized with respect to the proper integer norm.
AlgebraicStructureTraits_::DivMod.detailed: Note that the last condition is needed to ensure a unique computation of the pair $ (q,r)$ . However, an other option is to require minimality for $ |r|$ , with the advantage that a mod(x,y) operation would return the unique representative of the residue class of $ x$ with respect to $ y$ , e.g. $ mod(2,3)$ should return $ -1$ . But this conflicts with nearly all current implementation of integer types. From there, we decided to stay conform with common implementations and require $ q$ to be computed as $ x/y$ rounded towards zero. The following table illustrates the behavior for integers: x y q r 3 3 1 0 2 3 0 2 1 3 0 1 0 3 0 0 -1 3 0 -1 -2 3 0 -2 -3 3 -1 0 x y q r 3 -3 -1 0 2 -3 0 2 1 -3 0 1 0 -3 0 0 -1 -3 0 -1 -2 -3 0 -2 -3 -3 1 0 Refines AdaptableFunctor AlgebraicStructureTraits AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Mod AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Div

members.name: operator()
members.brief: computes the quotient $ q$ and remainder $ r$ , such that $ x = q*y + r$ and $ r$ minimal with respect to the Euclidean Norm on Type .
members.argsstring: (first_argument_type x, second_argument_type y, third_argument_type q, fourth_argument_type r)

members.name: operator()
members.brief: This operator is defined if NT1 and NT2 are ExplicitInteroperable with coercion type AlgebraicStructureTraits::Type .
members.argsstring: (NT1 x, NT2 y, third_argument_type q, fourth_argument_type r)

AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Gcd.name: AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Gcd
AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Gcd.detailed: The greatest common divisor ( $ gcd$ ) of ring elements $ x$ and $ y$ is the unique ring element $ d$ (up to a unit) with the property that any common divisor of $ x$ and $ y$ also divides $ d$ . (In other words: $ d$ is the greatest lower bound of $ x$ and $ y$ in the partial order of divisibility.) We demand the $ gcd$ to be unit-normal (i.e. have unit part 1). $ gcd(0,0)$ is defined as $ 0$ , since $ 0$ is the greatest element with respect to the partial order of divisibility. This is because an element $ a \in R$ is said to divide $ b \in R$ , iff $ \exists r \in R$ such that $ a \cdot r = b$ . Thus, $ 0$ is divided by every element of the Ring, in particular by itself. Refines AdaptableBinaryFunction AlgebraicStructureTraits

members.name: operator()
members.brief: This operator is defined if NT1 and NT2 are ExplicitInteroperable with coercion type AlgebraicStructureTraits::Type .

AlgebraicStructureTraits_::IntegralDivis.name: AlgebraicStructureTraits_::IntegralDivision
AlgebraicStructureTraits_::IntegralDivis.detailed: Integral division (a.k.a. exact division or division without remainder) maps ring elements $ (x,y)$ to ring element $ z$ such that $ x = yz$ if such a $ z$ exists (i.e. if $ x$ is divisible by $ y$ ). Otherwise the effect of invoking this operation is undefined. Since the ring represented is an integral domain, $ z$ is uniquely defined if it exists. Refines AdaptableBinaryFunction AlgebraicStructureTraits AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Divides

members.name: operator()
members.brief: returns $ x/y$ , this is an integral division.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: This operator is defined if NT1 and NT2 are ExplicitInteroperable with coercion type AlgebraicStructureTraits::Type .

AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Inverse.name: AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Inverse
AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Inverse.brief: AdaptableUnaryFunction providing the inverse element with respect to multiplication of a Field .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: returns the inverse element of $ x$ with respect to multiplication.

AlgebraicStructureTraits_::IsOne.name: AlgebraicStructureTraits_::IsOne
AlgebraicStructureTraits_::IsOne.brief: AdaptableUnaryFunction , returns true in case the argument is the one of the ring.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: returns true in case $ x$ is the one of the ring.

AlgebraicStructureTraits_::IsSquare.name: AlgebraicStructureTraits_::IsSquare
AlgebraicStructureTraits_::IsSquare.brief: AdaptableBinaryFunction that computes whether the first argument is a square. If the first argument is a square the second argument, which is taken by reference, contains the square root. Otherwise, the content of the second argument is undefined.
AlgebraicStructureTraits_::IsSquare.detailed: A ring element $ x$ is said to be a square iff there exists a ring element $ y$ such that $ x= y*y$ . In case the ring is a UniqueFactorizationDomain , $ y$ is uniquely defined up to multiplication by units. Refines AdaptableBinaryFunction AlgebraicStructureTraits

members.name: operator()
members.brief: returns true in case $ x$ is a square, i.e. $ x = y*y$ .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: returns true in case $ x$ is a square.

AlgebraicStructureTraits_::IsZero.name: AlgebraicStructureTraits_::IsZero
AlgebraicStructureTraits_::IsZero.brief: AdaptableUnaryFunction , returns true in case the argument is the zero element of the ring.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: returns true in case $ x$ is the zero element of the ring.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: returns the $ k$ -th root of $ x$ .

AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Mod.name: AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Mod
AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Mod.brief: AdaptableBinaryFunction computes the remainder of division with remainder.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: This operator is defined if NT1 and NT2 are ExplicitInteroperable with coercion type AlgebraicStructureTraits::Type .

AlgebraicStructureTraits_::RootOf.name: AlgebraicStructureTraits_::RootOf
AlgebraicStructureTraits_::RootOf.brief: AdaptableFunctor computes a real root of a square-free univariate polynomial.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: returns the k-th real root of the univariate polynomial, which is defined by the iterator range, where begin refers to the constant term.
members.detailed: The polynomial is square-free. The value type of the InputIterator is AlgebraicStructureTraits::Type .

AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Simplify.name: AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Simplify
AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Simplify.brief: This AdaptableUnaryFunction may simplify a given object.

AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Square.name: AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Square
AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Square.brief: AdaptableUnaryFunction , computing the square of the argument.

AlgebraicStructureTraits_::UnitPart.name: AlgebraicStructureTraits_::UnitPart
AlgebraicStructureTraits_::UnitPart.brief: This AdaptableUnaryFunction computes the unit part of a given ring element.
AlgebraicStructureTraits_::UnitPart.detailed: The mathematical definition of unit part is as follows: Two ring elements $ a$ and $ b$ are said to be associate if there exists an invertible ring element (i.e. a unit) $ u$ such that $ a = ub$ . This defines an equivalence relation. We can distinguish exactly one element of every equivalence class as being unit normal. Then each element of a ring possesses a factorization into a unit (called its unit part) and a unit-normal ring element (called its unit normal associate). For the integers, the non-negative numbers are by convention unit normal, hence the unit-part of a non-zero integer is its sign. For a Field , every non-zero element is a unit and is its own unit part, its unit normal associate being one. The unit part of zero is, by convention, one. Refines AdaptableUnaryFunction AlgebraicStructureTraits

members.name: operator()
members.brief: returns the unit part of $ x$ .

CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits.name: CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits
CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits.brief: An instance of Algebraic_structure_traits is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits , where T is the associated type.

CGAL::Coercion_traits.name: CGAL::Coercion_traits

members.detailed: This is either CGAL::Tag_true or CGAL::Tag_false .

members.name: Are_implicit_interoperable

members.name: Type
members.brief: The coercion type of A and B .
members.detailed: In case A and B are not ExplicitInteroperable this is undefined.

members.name: Cast
members.brief: A model of the AdaptableFunctor concept, providing the conversion of A or B to Type .
members.detailed: In case A and B are not ExplicitInteroperable this is undefined.

CGAL::Unique_factorization_domain_tag.brief: Tag indicating that a type is a model of the UniqueFactorizationDomain concept.

EuclideanRing.name: EuclideanRing
EuclideanRing.brief: A model of EuclideanRing represents a Euclidean ring (or Euclidean domain). It is an UniqueFactorizationDomain that affords a suitable notion of minimality of remainders such that given $ x$ and $ y \neq 0$ we obtain an (almost) unique solution to $ x = qy + r $ by demanding that a solution $ (q,r)$ is chosen to minimize $ r$ . In particular, $ r$ is chosen to be $ 0$ if possible.
EuclideanRing.detailed: Moreover, CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits < EuclideanRing > is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits providing: CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< EuclideanRing >::Algebraic_category derived from CGAL::Unique_factorization_domain_tag CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< EuclideanRing >::Mod which is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Mod CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< EuclideanRing >::Div which is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Div CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< EuclideanRing >::Div_mod which is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits_::DivMod Remarks The most prominent example of a Euclidean ring are the integers. Whenever both $ x$ and $ y$ are positive, then it is conventional to choose the smallest positive remainder $ r$ . Refines UniqueFactorizationDomain IntegralDomainWithoutDivision IntegralDomain UniqueFactorizationDomain EuclideanRing Field FieldWithSqrt FieldWithKthRoot FieldWithRootOf AlgebraicStructureTraits

ExplicitInteroperable.name: ExplicitInteroperable
ExplicitInteroperable.brief: Two types A and B are a model of the ExplicitInteroperable concept, if it is possible to derive a superior type for A and B , such that both types are embeddable into this type. This type is CGAL::Coercion_traits<A,B>::Type .
ExplicitInteroperable.detailed: In this case CGAL::Coercion_traits<A,B>::Are_explicit_interoperable is Tag_true . A and B are valid argument types for all binary functors in CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits <Type> and CGAL::Real_embeddable_traits <Type> . This is also the case for the respective global functions. CGAL::Coercion_traits <A,B> ImplicitInteroperable AlgebraicStructureTraits RealEmbeddableTraits

Field.name: Field
Field.brief: A model of Field is an IntegralDomain in which every non-zero element has a multiplicative inverse. Thus, one can divide by any non-zero element. Hence division is defined for any divisor != 0. For a Field , we require this division operation to be available through operators / and /=.
Field.detailed: Moreover, CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits < Field > is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits providing: CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< Field >::Algebraic_category derived from CGAL::Field_tag CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< FieldWithSqrt >::Inverse which is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Inverse Refines IntegralDomain IntegralDomainWithoutDivision IntegralDomain UniqueFactorizationDomain EuclideanRing Field FieldWithSqrt FieldWithKthRoot FieldWithRootOf AlgebraicStructureTraits

FieldNumberType.name: FieldNumberType
FieldNumberType.brief: The concept FieldNumberType combines the requirements of the concepts Field and RealEmbeddable . A model of FieldNumberType can be used as a template parameter for Cartesian kernels.
FieldNumberType.detailed: Refines Field RealEmbeddable Has models float double CGAL::Gmpq CGAL::Interval_nt CGAL::Interval_nt_advanced CGAL::Lazy_exact_nt< FieldNumberType > CGAL::Quotient< RingNumberType > leda_rational leda_bigfloat leda_real RingNumberType Kernel

FieldWithKthRoot.name: FieldWithKthRoot
FieldWithKthRoot.brief: A model of FieldWithKthRoot is a FieldWithSqrt that has operations to take k-th roots.
FieldWithKthRoot.detailed: Moreover, CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits < FieldWithKthRoot > is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits providing: CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< FieldWithKthRoot >::Algebraic_category derived from CGAL::Field_with_kth_root_tag CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< FieldWithKthRoot >::Kth_root which is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits_::KthRoot Refines FieldWithSqrt IntegralDomainWithoutDivision IntegralDomain UniqueFactorizationDomain EuclideanRing Field FieldWithSqrt FieldWithKthRoot FieldWithRootOf AlgebraicStructureTraits

FieldWithRootOf.name: FieldWithRootOf
FieldWithRootOf.brief: A model of FieldWithRootOf is a FieldWithKthRoot with the possibility to construct it as the root of a univariate polynomial.
FieldWithRootOf.detailed: Moreover, CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits < FieldWithRootOf > is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits providing: CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< FieldWithRootOf >::Algebraic_category derived from CGAL::Field_with_kth_root_tag CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< FieldWithRootOf >::Root_of which is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits_::RootOf Refines FieldWithKthRoot IntegralDomainWithoutDivision IntegralDomain UniqueFactorizationDomain EuclideanRing Field FieldWithSqrt FieldWithKthRoot FieldWithRootOf AlgebraicStructureTraits

FieldWithSqrt.name: FieldWithSqrt
FieldWithSqrt.brief: A model of FieldWithSqrt is a Field that has operations to take square roots.
FieldWithSqrt.detailed: Moreover, CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits < FieldWithSqrt > is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits providing: CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< FieldWithSqrt >::Algebraic_category derived from CGAL::Field_with_sqrt_tag CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< FieldWithSqrt >::Sqrt which is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Sqrt Refines Field IntegralDomainWithoutDivision IntegralDomain UniqueFactorizationDomain EuclideanRing Field FieldWithSqrt FieldWithKthRoot FieldWithRootOf AlgebraicStructureTraits

Fraction.name: Fraction
Fraction.brief: A type is considered as a Fraction , if there is a reasonable way to decompose it into a numerator and denominator. In this case the relevant functionality for decomposing and re-composing as well as the numerator and denominator type are provided by CGAL::Fraction_traits .

FractionTraits.name: FractionTraits
FractionTraits.brief: A model of FractionTraits is associated with a type Type .
FractionTraits.detailed: In case the associated type is a Fraction , a model of FractionTraits provides the relevant functionality for decomposing and re-composing as well as the numerator and denominator type. Has models CGAL::Fraction_traits <T> FractionTraits_::Decompose FractionTraits_::Compose FractionTraits_::CommonFactor

members.name: Is_fraction
members.brief: Tag indicating whether the associated type is a fraction and can be decomposed into a numerator and denominator.
members.detailed: This is either CGAL::Tag_true or CGAL::Tag_false .

members.name: Numerator_type
members.brief: The type to represent the numerator.
members.detailed: This is undefined in case the associated type is not a fraction.

members.name: Denominator_type
members.brief: The (simpler) type to represent the denominator.
members.detailed: This is undefined in case the associated type is not a fraction.

FractionTraits_::CommonFactor.name: FractionTraits_::CommonFactor
FractionTraits_::CommonFactor.brief: AdaptableBinaryFunction , finds great common factor of denominators.
FractionTraits_::CommonFactor.detailed: This can be considered as a relaxed version of AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Gcd , this is needed because it is not guaranteed that FractionTraits::Denominator_type is a model of UniqueFactorizationDomain . Refines AdaptableBinaryFunction Fraction FractionTraits FractionTraits_::Decompose FractionTraits_::Compose AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Gcd

members.name: operator()
members.brief: return a great common factor of $ d1$ and $ d2$ .

FractionTraits_::Compose.name: FractionTraits_::Compose
FractionTraits_::Compose.brief: AdaptableBinaryFunction , returns the fraction of its arguments.
FractionTraits_::Compose.detailed: Refines AdaptableBinaryFunction Fraction FractionTraits FractionTraits_::Decompose FractionTraits_::CommonFactor

FractionTraits_::Decompose.name: FractionTraits_::Decompose
FractionTraits_::Decompose.brief: Functor decomposing a Fraction into its numerator and denominator.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: decompose $ f$ into numerator $ n$ and denominator $ d$ .

FromDoubleConstructible.name: FromDoubleConstructible
FromDoubleConstructible.brief: A model of the concept FromDoubleConstructible is required to be constructible from the type double .
FromDoubleConstructible.detailed: In case the type is a model of RealEmbeddable too, for any double d the identity: d == CGAL::to_double(T(d)) , is guaranteed.

FromIntConstructible.name: FromIntConstructible
FromIntConstructible.brief: A model of the concept FromIntConstructible is required to be constructible from int.

ImplicitInteroperable.name: ImplicitInteroperable
ImplicitInteroperable.brief: Two types A and B are a model of the concept ImplicitInteroperable , if there is a superior type, such that binary arithmetic operations involving A and B result in this type. This type is CGAL::Coercion_traits<A,B>::Type . In case types are RealEmbeddable this also implies that mixed compare operators are available.
ImplicitInteroperable.detailed: The type CGAL::Coercion_traits<A,B>::Type is required to be implicit constructible from A and B . In this case CGAL::Coercion_traits<A,B>::Are_implicit_interoperable is CGAL::Tag_true . Refines ExplicitInteroperable CGAL::Coercion_traits <A,B> ExplicitInteroperable AlgebraicStructureTraits RealEmbeddableTraits

IntegralDomain.name: IntegralDomain
IntegralDomain.brief: IntegralDomain refines IntegralDomainWithoutDivision by providing an integral division.
IntegralDomain.detailed: Note: The concept does not require the operator / for this operation. We intend to reserve the operator syntax for use with a Field . Moreover, CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits < IntegralDomain > is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits providing: CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< IntegralDomain >::Algebraic_category derived from CGAL::Integral_domain_tag CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< IntegralDomain >::Integral_division which is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits_::IntegralDivision CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< IntegralDomain >::Divides which is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Divides Refines IntegralDomainWithoutDivision IntegralDomainWithoutDivision IntegralDomain UniqueFactorizationDomain EuclideanRing Field FieldWithSqrt FieldWithKthRoot FieldWithRootOf AlgebraicStructureTraits

IntegralDomainWithoutDivision.name: IntegralDomainWithoutDivision
IntegralDomainWithoutDivision.brief: This is the most basic concept for algebraic structures considered within CGAL.
IntegralDomainWithoutDivision.detailed: A model IntegralDomainWithoutDivision represents an integral domain, i.e. commutative ring with 0, 1, +, * and unity free of zero divisors. Note: A model is not required to offer the always well defined integral division. It refines Assignable , CopyConstructible , DefaultConstructible and FromIntConstructible . It refines EqualityComparable , where equality is defined w.r.t. the ring element being represented. The operators unary and binary plus +, unary and binary minus -, multiplication * and their compound forms +=, -=, *= are required and implement the respective ring operations. Moreover, CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits < IntegralDomainWithoutDivision > is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits providing: CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< IntegralDomainWithoutDivision >::Algebraic_category derived from CGAL::Integral_domain_without_division_tag CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< IntegralDomainWithoutDivision >::Is_zero which is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits_::IsZero CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< IntegralDomainWithoutDivision >::Is_one which is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits_::IsOne CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< IntegralDomainWithoutDivision >::Square which is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Square CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< IntegralDomainWithoutDivision >::Simplify which is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Simplify CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< IntegralDomainWithoutDivision >::Unit_part which is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits_::UnitPart Refines Assignable CopyConstructible DefaultConstructible EqualityComparable FromIntConstructible IntegralDomainWithoutDivision IntegralDomain UniqueFactorizationDomain EuclideanRing Field FieldWithSqrt FieldWithKthRoot FieldWithRootOf AlgebraicStructureTraits

members.name: operator==
members.brief: The result_type is convertible to bool .

members.name: operator!=
members.brief: The result_type is convertible to bool .

RealEmbeddable.name: RealEmbeddable
RealEmbeddable.brief: A model of this concepts represents numbers that are embeddable on the real axis. The type obeys the algebraic structure and compares two values according to the total order of the real numbers.
RealEmbeddable.detailed: Moreover, CGAL::Real_embeddable_traits < RealEmbeddable > is a model of RealEmbeddableTraits with: CGAL::Real_embeddable_traits< RealEmbeddable >::Is_real_embeddable set to Tag_true and functors : CGAL::Real_embeddable_traits< RealEmbeddable >::Is_zero which is a model of RealEmbeddableTraits_::IsZero CGAL::Real_embeddable_traits< RealEmbeddable >::Abs which is a model of RealEmbeddableTraits_::Abs CGAL::Real_embeddable_traits< RealEmbeddable >::Sgn which is a model of RealEmbeddableTraits_::Sgn CGAL::Real_embeddable_traits< RealEmbeddable >::Is_positive which is a model of RealEmbeddableTraits_::IsPositive CGAL::Real_embeddable_traits< RealEmbeddable >::Is_negative which is a model of RealEmbeddableTraits_::IsNegative CGAL::Real_embeddable_traits< RealEmbeddable >::Compare which is a model of RealEmbeddableTraits_::Compare CGAL::Real_embeddable_traits< RealEmbeddable >::To_double which is a model of RealEmbeddableTraits_::ToDouble CGAL::Real_embeddable_traits< RealEmbeddable >::To_interval which is a model of RealEmbeddableTraits_::ToInterval Remark: If a number type is a model of both IntegralDomainWithoutDivision and RealEmbeddable , it follows that the ring represented by such a number type is a sub-ring of the real numbers and hence has characteristic zero. Refines EqualityComparable LessThanComparable RealEmbeddableTraits

RealEmbeddableTraits.name: RealEmbeddableTraits
RealEmbeddableTraits.brief: A model of RealEmbeddableTraits is associated to a number type Type and reflects the properties of this type with respect to the concept RealEmbeddable .

members.name: Is_real_embeddable
members.brief: Tag indicating whether the associated type is real embeddable.
members.detailed: This is either Tag_true or Tag_false .

members.name: Boolean
members.brief: This type specifies the return type of the predicates provided by this traits.
members.detailed: The type must be convertible to bool and typically the type indeed maps to bool . However, there are also cases such as interval arithmetic, in which it is Uncertain<bool> or some similar type.

members.name: Sign
members.brief: This type specifies the return type of the Sgn functor.
members.detailed: The type must be convertible to CGAL::Sign and typically the type indeed maps to CGAL::Sign . However, there are also cases such as interval arithmetic, in which it is Uncertain<CGAL::Sign> or some similar type.

members.name: Comparison_result
members.brief: This type specifies the return type of the Compare functor.
members.detailed: The type must be convertible to CGAL::Comparison_result and typically the type indeed maps to CGAL::Comparison_result . However, there are also cases such as interval arithmetic, in which it is Uncertain<CGAL::Comparison_result> or some similar type.

members.name: Is_zero
members.brief: A model of RealEmbeddableTraits_::IsZero In case Type is also model of IntegralDomainWithoutDivision this is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits_::IsZero .

RealEmbeddableTraits_::Abs.name: RealEmbeddableTraits_::Abs
RealEmbeddableTraits_::Abs.brief: AdaptableUnaryFunction computes the absolute value of a number.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: computes the absolute value of $ x$ .

RealEmbeddableTraits_::Compare.name: RealEmbeddableTraits_::Compare
RealEmbeddableTraits_::Compare.brief: AdaptableBinaryFunction compares two real embeddable numbers.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: compares $ x$ with respect to $ y$ .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: This operator is defined if NT1 and NT2 are ExplicitInteroperable with coercion type RealEmbeddableTraits::Type .

RealEmbeddableTraits_::IsNegative.name: RealEmbeddableTraits_::IsNegative
RealEmbeddableTraits_::IsNegative.brief: AdaptableUnaryFunction , returns true in case the argument is negative.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: returns true in case $ x$ is negative.

RealEmbeddableTraits_::IsPositive.name: RealEmbeddableTraits_::IsPositive
RealEmbeddableTraits_::IsPositive.brief: AdaptableUnaryFunction , returns true in case the argument is positive.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: returns true in case $ x$ is positive.

RealEmbeddableTraits_::IsZero.name: RealEmbeddableTraits_::IsZero
RealEmbeddableTraits_::IsZero.brief: AdaptableUnaryFunction , returns true in case the argument is 0.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: returns true in case $ x$ is the zero element of the ring.

RealEmbeddableTraits_::Sgn.name: RealEmbeddableTraits_::Sgn
RealEmbeddableTraits_::Sgn.brief: This AdaptableUnaryFunction computes the sign of a real embeddable number.

RealEmbeddableTraits_::ToDouble.name: RealEmbeddableTraits_::ToDouble
RealEmbeddableTraits_::ToDouble.brief: AdaptableUnaryFunction computes a double approximation of a real embeddable number.
RealEmbeddableTraits_::ToDouble.detailed: Remark: In order to control the quality of approximation one has to resort to methods that are specific to NT. There are no general guarantees whatsoever. Refines AdaptableUnaryFunction RealEmbeddableTraits

members.name: operator()
members.brief: computes a double approximation of a real embeddable number.

RealEmbeddableTraits_::ToInterval.name: RealEmbeddableTraits_::ToInterval
RealEmbeddableTraits_::ToInterval.brief: AdaptableUnaryFunction computes for a given real embeddable number $ x$ a double interval containing $ x$ . This interval is represented by std::pair<double,double> .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: computes a double interval containing $ x$ .

RingNumberType.name: RingNumberType
RingNumberType.brief: The concept RingNumberType combines the requirements of the concepts IntegralDomainWithoutDivision and RealEmbeddable . A model of RingNumberType can be used as a template parameter for homogeneous kernels.
RingNumberType.detailed: Refines IntegralDomainWithoutDivision RealEmbeddable Has models C++ built-in number types CGAL::Gmpq CGAL::Gmpz CGAL::Interval_nt CGAL::Interval_nt_advanced CGAL::Lazy_exact_nt< RingNumberType > CGAL::MP_Float CGAL::Gmpzf CGAL::Quotient< RingNumberType > leda_integer leda_rational leda_bigfloat leda_real FieldNumberType

UniqueFactorizationDomain.name: UniqueFactorizationDomain
UniqueFactorizationDomain.brief: A model of UniqueFactorizationDomain is an IntegralDomain with the additional property that the ring it represents is a unique factorization domain (a.k.a. UFD or factorial ring), meaning that every non-zero non-unit element has a factorization into irreducible elements that is unique up to order and up to multiplication by invertible elements (units). (An irreducible element is a non-unit ring element that cannot be factored further into two non-unit elements. In a UFD, the irreducible elements are precisely the prime elements.).
UniqueFactorizationDomain.detailed: In a UFD, any two elements, not both zero, possess a greatest common divisor (gcd). Moreover, CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits < UniqueFactorizationDomain > is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits providing: CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< UniqueFactorizationDomain >::Algebraic_category derived from CGAL::Unique_factorization_domain_tag CGAL::Algebraic_structure_traits< UniqueFactorizationDomain >::Gcd which is a model of AlgebraicStructureTraits_::Gcd Refines IntegralDomain IntegralDomainWithoutDivision IntegralDomain UniqueFactorizationDomain EuclideanRing Field FieldWithSqrt FieldWithKthRoot FieldWithRootOf AlgebraicStructureTraits
